name: wilson-lsp
description: Wires LSP servers for the dancinlab formats into Claude Code via .lsp.json: .hexa (and the firmware-authoring extensions hexa absorbs — .c .h .cpp .hpp .cc .hh / .s .S / .v .sv .vhd .vhdl) → `hexa lsp`; and .tape / .n6 / .hxc / .kosmos → the canonical per-repo servers (tape-lsp / n6-lsp / hxc-lsp / kosmos-lsp, shipped in github.com/dancinlab/{tape,n6,hxc,kosmos}). The firmware-ext routing matches wilson-hexa-first-warn's protected set — opening a .c/.v file gets hexa-lsp diagnostics so the .hexa-is-the-replacement signal lands at the LSP layer too. .py/.sh are deliberately NOT claimed (existing pyright/bash-language-server conflict). Graceful: any server not on PATH simply shows in the /plugin Errors tab. Not a hook plugin — LSP lifecycle is CC-managed.
